# UC-22.2.19 · NIS2 Art.23(3) — Cross-Border Incident Impact Assessment Completeness

## Description

Detects significant ES notables whose source, destination, or impacted asset country indicates cross-border impact, and either no Art.23(3) cross-border impact assessment artefact has been filed yet (T-6h notice) or has been pending for >18h (overdue).

## Value

NIS2 Art.23(3) requires the operator to inform other affected member-state CSIRTs of cross-border incidents — without an impact assessment the operator cannot identify those CSIRTs. This UC ensures the Comms/CSIRT-liaison team gets a tasking signal as soon as cross-border indicators appear, before the 24h early-warning deadline closes.

## Implementation

(1) Confirm `assets_by_str.csv` has `country` populated for at least 95% of in-scope assets. (2) Schedule every 30 minutes over 7 days. (3) Page CSIRT liaison on `assessment_overdue=1`; email digest on `assessment_status=PENDING` with `hours_since_detection > 6`. (4) Configure SOAR to write `stage=crossborder_assessment` artefact with `member_states_affected` field on completion. (5) Throttle by `event_id` for 12 hours.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• UC-22.2.1 implemented (24h clock for significant incidents).
• Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263) ≥7.0 with Asset and Identity Framework configured to populate `country`, `business_unit`, and `criticality` per asset (typically sourced from ServiceNow CMDB).
• Splunk built-in `iplocation` command available (no add-on required); GeoIP database current (within 30 days).
• Splunk Add-on for Phantom (Splunkbase 3411) ≥3.0 if SOAR is in the path.
• Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (Splunkbase 1621) ≥5.3 for Network_Traffic acceleration.
• Lookups: `nis2_significant_incident_thresholds.csv`, `nis2_entity_scope.csv` (operator's regulated entities and their countries), `aws_cidr_ranges.csv`, `azure_cidr_ranges.csv`, `cdn_edge_ranges.csv`, `nis2_cloud_hosted_assets.csv`, `nis2_internal_vpn_egress.csv`, `hr_travel.csv`.
• Roles: `read` on `index=notable`, `incident_review` KV-store, `index=audit_evidence`, `read` on the asset/identity lookups.
• Network: same SH that runs UC-22.2.1.
• License: ~6 KB per finding row (extra fields). Steady-state ~5–20 rows/day.
• Baseline knowledge: which member states the operator is registered in (sourced from `nis2_entity_scope.csv`); the operator's policy on whose CSIRT gets notified for which kind of incident.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
• Confirm Asset and Identity Framework populates `country`: `| inputlookup assets_by_str.csv | stats count by country | where isnull(country)`. Should be < 5% of assets; populate via ServiceNow CMDB extended fields.
• Confirm GeoIP database freshness: `| makeresults | eval src="8.8.8.8" | iplocation src | table Country City` should return `United States, Mountain View` (or similar). Outdated DBs return `Unknown` for recent IP allocations.
• Confirm cloud-CIDR lookups are current: `| inputlookup aws_cidr_ranges.csv | stats max(date_synced)` should be within 7 days. AWS publishes CIDR updates weekly via `https://ip-ranges.amazonaws.com/ip-ranges.json`.
• Configure SOAR or IR-portal automation to write `stage=crossborder_assessment` artefact with `member_states_affected` (CSV list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 codes), `assessor`, `decision` (notify | not_notify | escalate_to_legal).
• Expected event volume: 1 finding row per cross-border significant notable per polling cycle. Steady state: 5–20 rows/day for a multi-state operator.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Use the `spl` field above. Save as scheduled search `UC-22.2.19 NIS2 Art.23 Cross-Border Posture`.

Understanding this SPL — design choices:
• **`iplocation` on both src and dest** so any cross-state network indicator is captured; CSIRTs care about ingress AND egress.
• **Asset country from `assets_by_str.csv`** because the asset's regulated entity may be in a different country than the IP's geolocation (cloud hosting).
• **`distinct_countries > 1` as the cross-border test** rather than `country != operator_home_country` — because operators with multi-state regulated entities have no single 'home country'.
• **18h overdue threshold** (vs the 24h hard deadline) gives the CSIRT liaison 6 hours of buffer to draft and submit the notification.
• **`mvdedup(mvappend(...))`** to avoid double-counting when src_country == asset_country.
• **`mvfilter` regex** drops empty country codes that come from `Unknown`/null GeoIP results.

Schedule and alert action:
• Cron: `*/30 * * * *` (every 30 min). Time range `-7d to now`.
• Alert action: `Number of results > 0`. Throttle by `event_id` for 12 hours.
• Notification: PagerDuty severity `warning` for new cross-border findings, `critical` for `assessment_overdue=1`. Annotate with the `crossborder_indicators` string and the deep link to ES Incident Review.
• Summary indexing: write findings to `index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:saved_search`.

Step 3 — Validate
• Compare to ES Incident Review: filter by Severity=High,Critical, last 24h, and inspect the notable's contributing fields. The cross-border notables should have non-EU `src` or `dest` IPs; manually confirm geolocation via Entra/Okta sign-in logs OR via a third-party WHOIS for two of them.
• Spot-check the GeoIP DB: pick two recent notables with known src IP geography (e.g. employee VPN egress from Stockholm) and confirm `iplocation src` returns Sweden.
• Cross-check with Splunk Network Resolution data: for two cross-border notables, the corresponding `index=dns` queries (UC-22.2.55) should resolve to the same destination country.
• Confirm assessment-write hook: trigger a synthetic SOAR playbook to write a `stage=crossborder_assessment` artefact; confirm the SPL flips that incident from PENDING to FILED in <30 seconds.
• Audit-evidence completeness: `index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:nis2_artifact stage=crossborder_assessment | head 5 | spath member_states_affected | table _time member_states_affected assessor decision` — every artefact should have all four fields populated.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard `compliance/nis2-art23-posture.xml` (Cross-border tab):
• **Row 1 — Tiles:** Pending assessments, Overdue assessments, Average distinct_countries.
• **Row 2 — Geo-map:** Current cross-border posture; plot incidents on a map where the size of the marker is `distinct_countries` and the colour is `urgency`.
• **Row 3 — Country-pair matrix:** 30-day heatmap of source-country × destination-country cross-border incident counts. High-traffic pairs reveal policy gaps (e.g. NL↔DE production traffic that should be classified as internal).
• **Row 4 — Sortable table:** rule_name, distinct_countries, crossborder_indicators, hours_since_detection, owner, drilldown to ES Incident Review.
• **Time picker:** Default `Last 4 hours` (active triage); `Last 30 days` (review).
• **Access:** `nis2_evidence_reader`, `ir_analyst`, `head_of_ir`, `csirt_liaison`, `legal_grc`.

Runbook (owner: CSIRT Liaison, reports to Head of IR):
1. PagerDuty page on `assessment_overdue=1`. CSIRT liaison opens the ES Incident Review notable and the SOAR case via deep-link.
2. Confirm cross-border indicators are real (not GeoIP noise — see KFPs). Use the geo-map panel to visualise.
3. Cross-reference `nis2_entity_scope.csv` to determine which member-state CSIRTs the operator MUST notify under Art.23(3).
4. Draft the cross-border notification (typically a copy of the 24h early-warning, with an explicit `affected_member_states` list and proposed coordination protocol).
5. Submit to each affected CSIRT via the secure channel; record the assessment in the IR portal, which writes the `stage=crossborder_assessment` artefact.
6. Capacity-review monthly: `| stats count by crossborder_indicators | sort - count | head 10` reveals which country pairs are recurring.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **All notables show `distinct_countries=1` even when src and dest are clearly different countries.** GeoIP database is stale or missing. Confirm with `| makeresults | eval ip="1.1.1.1" | iplocation ip` returns `Australia, Sydney`. Refresh via `splunk maxmind` if licensed; alternative: use the Splunk Common Information Model Add-on bundled GeoLite2 update.
• **`asset_country` is null for most rows.** `assets_by_str.csv` does not have `country` populated. Update the ES Asset and Identity Framework's CMDB pull to include the country field; refresh via `Settings > Data Enrichment > Asset and Identity Configuration > Run merge job`.
• **Cloud-hosted assets always show as the cloud region, not the entity country.** Build `nis2_cloud_hosted_assets.csv` mapping `(asset_name, cloud_region)` to `treat_as_country=<entity_country>`. Update the SPL with a lookup override for cloud assets.
• **CDN traffic creates noisy cross-border findings.** Maintain `cdn_edge_ranges.csv` (CIDR ranges for Akamai, Cloudflare, Fastly, AWS CloudFront, Azure CDN) and filter dest IPs in those ranges OR resolve to the actual destination via SNI/Host header.
• **Multi-tenant SaaS shows every tenant's incident as cross-border.** Filter on `business_unit IN (operator_tenant_list)` from `nis2_entity_scope.csv`; the operator is one tenant of the SaaS, not the SaaS itself.
• **`mvjoin` returns empty even with non-empty fields.** `Country` from `iplocation` is sometimes a multi-value field if the IP is multi-homed; use `mvdedup(mvappend(...))` not `mvjoin(mvappend(...))`. The SPL above already does this.
• **Assessment-write hook works but `member_states_affected` field is empty in the artefact.** SOAR playbook is writing the artefact before the assessor completes the form. Add a playbook step to wait for `decision_recorded=true` before writing the artefact.
• **`crossborder_indicators` shows duplicate countries.** Multiple `mvappend` outputs include the same country code (e.g. src and asset both DE). The SPL uses `mvdedup` — confirm the dedup is applied AFTER the mvappend.

## SPL

```spl
`notable` urgency IN ("high","critical") earliest=-7d
| lookup nis2_significant_incident_thresholds.csv rule_name OUTPUT nis2_tier nis2_significant
| where coalesce(nis2_significant, "unclassified") != "no" AND status != "Closed"
| iplocation src
| rename Country as src_country
| iplocation dest
| rename Country as dest_country
| lookup assets_by_str.csv asset as risk_object OUTPUT country as asset_country, business_unit, criticality
| eval crossborder_indicators = mvjoin(mvdedup(mvfilter(match(src_country."|".dest_country."|".asset_country, "^[A-Z][A-Z]"))), ",")
| eval distinct_countries = mvcount(mvdedup(mvappend(src_country, dest_country, asset_country)))
| eval is_crossborder = if(distinct_countries > 1, 1, 0)
| where is_crossborder = 1
| eval hours_since_detection = round((now() - _time) / 3600, 2)
| join type=left rule_name event_id [
    search index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:nis2_artifact stage=crossborder_assessment earliest=-7d
    | stats latest(_time) as assessment_filed_time, latest(member_states_affected) as ms_affected by rule_name event_id
  ]
| eval assessment_status = if(isnotnull(assessment_filed_time), "FILED", "PENDING")
| eval assessment_overdue = if(assessment_status = "PENDING" AND hours_since_detection > 18, 1, 0)
| where assessment_status = "PENDING" OR assessment_overdue = 1
| eval evidence_id = sha256(rule_name . event_id . _time . "crossborder")
| table _time, rule_name, urgency, nis2_tier, owner, hours_since_detection, distinct_countries, crossborder_indicators, assessment_status, assessment_overdue, evidence_id
| sort - hours_since_detection
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.action="allowed" AND _time >= relative_time(now(), "-7d") by All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest
| iplocation All_Traffic.src
| iplocation All_Traffic.dest
| stats values(Country) as countries by All_Traffic.src
```

## Visualization

Single value: pending cross-border assessments. Geo-map (countries involved). Sortable table with deep links. Trellis: 30-day cross-border incident trend per country pair.
